Tshisekedi's potential third term bid could exacerbate DRC's political instability, affecting regional security and global resource markets.

articles.title: Nigeria pledges to strengthen bilateral cooperation with India
articles.excerpt:
Nigeria reaffirms its commitment to strengthening bilateral cooperation with India, focusing on key sectors like trade, energy, and security.

articles.title: World Cup Organizer Issues Update On ICE Presence At Stadiums
articles.excerpt: "This is not going to turn into some ‘round them up' type of thing. That's not the purpose of this."
articles.content:
U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement will not be seen at World Cup matches this summer, according to the co-chair of the Miami host committee.

articles.title: India labour market remodels itself bit by bit as agri slowly cedes ground, workforce moves on
articles.excerpt: India's labour market is undergoing a structural transformation driven by the country's broader economic transition, according to a report by SBI Research based on the latest Periodic Labour Force Survey (PLFS) 2025 unit-level data.
articles.content: India's labour market is undergoing a structural transformation driven by the country's broader economic transition, according to a report by SBI Research based on the latest Periodic Labour Force Survey (PLFS) 2025 unit-level data.

articles.title: 20 hikers missing after volcano erupts in east Indonesia: rescue official
articles.excerpt: Twenty hikers, nine of them from Singapore, were reported missing Friday after Mount Dukono on Indonesia's eastern Halmahera island erupted, spewing a smoke cloud some 10 kilometres (6.2 miles) into the air, a rescue official told. "Our team is on its way. La…

articles.title: Third British national has suspected hantavirus infection, government says
articles.excerpt: The patient is on the remote Atlantic island of Tristan da Cunha, which was visited by the cruise ship in April.
articles.content: Symptoms can include fever, extreme fatigue, stomach pain, vomiting and shortness of breath, usually appearing between two to four weeks after being exposed to the virus.
articles.author: BBC News
